message.content:
너는 AAD(AI-Agent Dashboard) 프로젝트의 **디자인 시스템 전면 교체 — 파운데이션 담당(에이전트 A)** 개발자다. 작업 루트: /Users/won-yechan/miru_github/agent-dashboard

## 배경
사용자가 Claude 디자인으로 만든 Apple 스타일 목업을 제공했고, "왠만하면 이 디자인을 그대로" 적용하기로 확정됐다. 앱은 로컬 전용(127.0.0.1) 대시보드로, 뷰는 개요·지시문·스킬·사용량 4개(에이전트/스토어/플레이그라운드 섹션은 hidden 존치)다. 너는 1단계로 **dashboard/design-system.css 전면 재작성 + dashboard/index.html 셸·정적 마크업 갱신**만 담당한다. app.js 렌더 함수는 다음 에이전트(B)가 맡으므로 **dashboard/app.js는 절대 수정하지 마라**. A 완료 시점에 동적 뷰 내용 일부가 아직 옛 마크업이라 어색해도 허용된다(커밋 없음).

## 필독 디자인 소스 (권위 순)
1. `/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-won-yechan-miru-github-agent-dashboard/93f87495-c8f3-48a0-bd2a-8771b089655c/scratchpad/body-skeleton.html` — 목업 전체 마크업(사이드바·개요·스킬·지시문·사용량·diff 모달·SVG 아이콘 전부). **마크업/아이콘의 권위 소스. 반드시 정독.**
2. 같은 폴더 `design-source.txt` — 계산형 스타일(navStyle/chipStyle/decorate/decLine/modalVals/sidebarStyle 등). **동적 스타일 값의 권위 소스.**
3. 현재 파일: dashboard/index.html(373줄), dashboard/design-system.css(1345줄) — 기존 클래스/ID 계약 파악용.

## 디자인 토큰 (CSS 변수로 정의)
- 모델색(헤더·범례 전용): Claude `#6D51D6`/ink `#5B3FC4`/head-bg `#F5F1FD`/border `#EBE3FA` · Codex `#0F7E88`/ink `#0B6771`/head-bg `#E9F4F5`/border `#D3EBEC`
- 상태색(배지 전용): 동기화됨 `#1E824C`/bg `#E6F4EB`/dot `#25a25a` · 불일치 `#8A5300`/bg `#FBEED6`/dot `#D79A2B` · 단독 `#5E5E63`/bg `#EFEFF1`/dot `#c7c7cc`
- 액션 파랑 `#0066CC` 하나 · 잉크 `#1d1d1f` · 보더 `#e0e0e0`/`#ededf0`/`#f0f0f2`/`#f2f2f4` · 표면 `#fff`/`#fbfbfd`/`#f7f7f9`
- 경고 배너: bg `#FBF5E9` border `#ECDDBC` 아이콘원 `#F3E4C2` 서브텍스트 `#8a7c58`
- diff: 다른 줄 `#FBF3E0` · 갭 해칭 `repeating-linear-gradient(45deg,#fafafb,#fafafb 6px,#f1f1f4 6px,#f1f1f4 12px)` · +줄 `#E9F6EE`/`#1E824C` · −줄 `#FBECEC`/`#B3300B`
- 타이포: 시스템 스택(-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont…; Inter 임베드 금지). h1 28/600/-0.5px · 섹션 16/600 · 본문 14 · 캡션 12 · 경로/diff ui-monospace 12.5 · 숫자 tabular-nums · weight 400/600/700만
- 반경: 카드 16 · 모달 18 · 코드블록 12 · 칩/버튼/배지/입력 pill(9999px)
- 사이드바: 펼침 256px/접힘 68px(아이콘 레일), bg `#fbfbfd`, transition width .18s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1). nav 항목: padding 9px 12px, radius 11px, 15px/-0.2px, 활성 bg `#EEF0F3`+border rgba(0,0,0,.06)+600
- 칩: 활성 `#1d1d1f` bg·흰 글자·600 / 비활성 흰 bg+`#e0e0e0` 보더 / 미정 dashed `#dadade`+`#a1a1a6`, 개수 스팬은 활성 rgba(255,255,255,.55)·비활성 `#b8b8bd`
- 다크모드 없음(라이트 고정). 기존 CSS 변수명은 유지 가능하면 유지하되 값 교체, 새 토큰(모델/상태/배너/diff)은 명확한 이름으로 추가.

## index.html 작업 (기존 파일 기반 수정)
1. **사이드바**: 목업 그대로 — AAD 사각 로고(34px, `#1d1d1f`, radius 9), 타이틀 "AI-Agent Dashboard"+서브 "로컬 전용 · Claude · Codex", 접기 버튼(SVG ‹), "탐색" 그룹 라벨, nav 4항목(개요/지시문/스킬/사용량)에 **목업의 인라인 SVG 아이콘**(body-skeleton.html에서 복사) + 기존 `.nav-count` 배지 유지. 푸터: 초록 점(글로우)+127.0.0.1·연결됨, 자물쇠 SVG+"로컬 전용 · 외부 전송 없음". 접힘 상태(body 또는 .app에 클래스)에서 아이콘만 중앙 정렬. **목업의 "리디자인 스펙" nav 항목은 넣지 않는다.**
2. **topnav(<1024 가로 메뉴) 완전 삭제** — app.js는 클릭 위임이라 안전함(검증됨). 반응형은 아이콘 레일로 대체.
3. **.topbar → 스티키 블러 헤더**: 28px/600/-0.5px 제목(#topbar-title) + 14px 서브(#topbar-sub), 우측 원형(36px) 새로고침 버튼(#refresh, SVG ↻), bg rgba(255,255,255,.9)+backdrop-filter blur(12px) saturate(180%), border-bottom `#f0f0f2`, padding 30px 40px 18px 급.
4. **개요 섹션 재구성** (모든 기존 id 반드시 존치 — app.js가 참조): `#ov-onboard`(hidden 유지), `#ov-guide`(→ B가 실행형 배너 렌더), `#ov-stats`(4열 grid), 신규 `#ov-grid1`(grid 1.35fr 1fr, gap 15px — 동기화 상태 카드 + 카테고리 TOP5 카드 자리, **`#ov-dist`는 ov-grid1 안 TOP5 카드 본문으로 존치**), 신규 `#ov-grid2`(grid 1.35fr 1fr — 최근 사용 + 모델 커버리지 자리, B가 채움). 기존 .bento 마크업 제거.
5. **스킬 섹션**: 기존 구조·id 전부 유지하되 목업 구조 반영 — 카테고리 칩 줄(#skills-filters)+프로젝트 관리 버튼(dark-utility 스타일, 폴더 SVG), 상태 칩+우측 270px 검색(#skill-mx-search), 신규 **범례 줄 `#skill-legend`**("현재 N / M개 표시 중 · ✓ 있음 · – 없음 · ■Claude ■Codex" — 개수는 B가 갱신, 정적 틀만), 폴더 패널(212px, sticky top 112px), 테이블 헤더 th에 모델색 사각점(7px, radius 2) 추가: `<th>…Claude</th>`를 목업처럼. 배치바(전체 선택/선택 항목 동기화)는 테이블 카드 상단 바(bg `#fbfbfd`)로 스타일. 에이전트 섹션(hidden)도 동일 클래스라 자연 상속 — 마크업 손대지 않아도 됨(원하면 th 점만 동일 적용).
6. **지시문 섹션**: `#instr-summary`(배너 컨테이너)·`#instr-cards`(좌우 카드 컨테이너) id 유지. 정적 틀만 목업 톤으로.
7. **사용량 섹션**: 신규 정적 요소 — 프라이버시 배너(자물쇠 SVG + 기존 `#usage-note` 텍스트를 배너 안으로 이동, id 유지), 신규 `#usage-stats`(3열 grid, B가 채움), 신규 정렬 토글 컨테이너 `#usage-sort`(B가 채움), 테이블 thead를 5컬럼(이름/최근 사용/지난 1일/지난 7일/전체)으로 교체, `#usage-rows` 유지.
8. **모달·뷰어·토스트**: id/구조 유지(sync-modal, conflict-modal, projects-modal, registries-modal, content-viewer, toast 등), 스타일만 CSS에서 새 언어로(모달 radius 18, 헤더 border-bottom `#f0f0f2`, 원형 닫기 버튼, pill 버튼).
9. 문구 교정: "세 모델의 설정을 한눈에" → "두 모델(Claude · Codex)의 설정을 한눈에". 남은 이모지 아이콘(🏠📄🧩📊🔎↻🔒📁 등)은 목업 SVG로 교체(검색 인풋의 🔎 포함).

## design-system.css 작업 (전면 재작성)
- 위 토큰 + 목업의 모든 컴포넌트: 사이드바/nav/아이콘레일, 스티키 헤더, 스탯 카드(38px 숫자), 경고 배너, 상태 배지(pill+dot), 칩, 버튼 3종(primary 파랑 pill `#0066CC`·secondary 파랑 아웃라인 pill·dark-utility `#1d1d1f` radius 8), 검색 인풋(pill, 38px), 폴더 패널·리스트, 매트릭스 테이블(행 44–52px, padding 11px 20px, border-bottom `#f2f2f4`, hover, **불일치 행 bg `#FCF8EE`**, **선택 행 bg `#F2F7FE`**, ✓ 마크 13.5px/700/`#1d1d1f`, – 마크 15px/400/`#cfcfd4`), 지시문 좌우 카드(모델색 헤더)+diff 줄(34px 줄번호 그리드, 12.5px mono, line-height 1.55), 사용량 테이블(5컬럼 grid 또는 table, 막대 트랙 `#f0f0f2`), 모달(18px, max-width 640, scrim rgba(20,20,22,.42)+blur(3px), 세그먼트 컨트롤: bg `#f0f0f2` radius 10 padding 4, 활성 흰 bg+shadow), 뷰어(전체 유지, 새 톤), 토스트.
- 기존 app.js가 쓰는 클래스명(예: .btn .btn-primary/.btn-secondary/.btn-ghost/.btn-sm, .filters .chip?, .items, .mx-table, .tag-badge?, .skel, .search, .modal-scrim/.modal, .viewer-*, .toast, .nav-item/.nav-count, .master-detail/.md-folders/.mdf-*/.md-detail, .mx-*, .batch-*, .stat-* 등)은 **grep으로 실제 사용처를 확인**해서 스타일이 끊기지 않게 같은 이름으로 새 디자인을 입혀라. app.js에서 `grep -oE 'class="[^"]+"' dashboard/app.js | sort -u`로 생성 클래스 목록을 뽑아 커버리지를 확인할 것.
- 반응형(데스크톱만): ≥1200 기본(사이드바 펼침+스킬 2단) · 1000–1200 폴더 패널이 매트릭스 위 스택(폴더 리스트 `repeat(auto-fill,minmax(160px,1fr))` 그리드, sticky 해제) · <1000 사이드바 자동 아이콘 레일(라벨 숨김)+지시문 좌우→상하 스택. 모바일 탭바·카드형 매트릭스는 구현하지 않음.
- 접힘은 두 경로: 사용자가 #sidebar-toggle 클릭(app.js가 클래스 토글 — 기존 toggleSidebar 로직이 쓰는 클래스명을 app.js에서 확인해 동일하게 지원) + <1000 미디어쿼리 자동.

## 준수 계약 (절대 어기지 말 것)
- **dashboard/app.js 수정 금지.** 다음 id 전부 존치: nav, sidebar-toggle, topbar-title, topbar-sub, refresh, global-search, global-search-input, ov-onboard, ov-guide, ov-stats, ov-dist, instr-summary, instr-cards, skills-filters, skill-folder-pane/skill-folder-search/skill-folder-list, skill-mx-state/skill-mx-search/skill-mx-context/skill-mx-scan/skill-mx-all/skill-mx-batch-sync/skill-mx-progress/skill-mx-table/skill-mx-rows/skill-mx-more/skill-mx-batch-result, agent-* 동등물, store-*·pg-* (hidden 섹션 내), usage-note/usage-rows, panel/scrim/panel-title/panel-meta/panel-body/panel-foot/panel-close, conflict-*·sync-*·projects-*·registries-* 모달 id 전부, content-viewer/viewer-modal/viewer-title/viewer-path/viewer-copy/viewer-tabs/viewer-close/viewer-body, toast/toast-msg.
- section[data-panel="…"] 래퍼와 .nav-item[data-view] 구조 유지(라우팅 계약).
- 새 컨테이너 id는 정확히: `#ov-grid1` `#ov-grid2` `#skill-legend` `#usage-stats` `#usage-sort` (B와의 계약).

## 보안 (불변)
실제 홈(~/.claude 등)·실제 canonical·backups에 쓰지 마라. 네트워크 접근 금지. cli/·server/·bin/ 코드 수정 금지. 보고서에 실제 사용자 파일 본문·개인정보·토큰 인용 금지. 서버 실행이 필요하면 반드시 **더미 모드 임시 포트만**: `AAD_MODE=dummy AAD_PORT=4321 node server/serve.js` 식으로(실제 모드 금지), 끝나면 프로세스 종료. 더미 서버에서도 쓰기 동작(sync 적용 등) 호출 금지 — GET 확인만.

## 검증 (완료 전 필수)
1. `node -c` 해당 없음(CSS/HTML) — 대신 index.html의 id 계약을 grep으로 전수 확인(위 목록이 모두 존재).
2. 더미 서버 임시 포트로 띄워 `curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://127.0.0.1:4321/` 및 `/design-system.css` 200 확인 후 종료.
3. CSS에 gemini 흔적·다크모드 미디어쿼리가 없는지 grep.
4. app.js 생성 클래스 커버리지 체크 결과(스타일 정의 없는 클래스가 남으면 목록으로 보고 — B가 마크업을 바꿀 예정이므로 참고용).

## 보고 형식 (최종 텍스트 = 데이터)
- 변경 파일과 핵심 구조(새 토큰 변수명 목록, 새 컨테이너 id, 삭제한 것: topnav 등)
- B에게 넘길 계약 요약: B가 렌더 시 써야 할 클래스명 사전(칩/배지/카드/행/마크/배너/세그먼트 등 — 이름과 용도 1줄씩). **이 사전이 가장 중요하다.**
- 검증 결과(200 확인, id 전수 확인, 미커버 클래스 목록)
- 판단이 필요했던 결정 사항

attachment.content:
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- honcho:config: Configure Honcho memory plugin settings interactively
- honcho:interview: Interview the user to capture stable, cross-project preferences and save them to Honcho
- honcho:setup: First-time Honcho configuration -- set API key, validate connection, create config
- honcho:status: Show current Honcho memory status and configuration
- understand-anything:understand: Analyze a codebase to produce an interactive knowledge graph for understanding architecture, components, and relationships
- understand-anything:understand-chat: Use when you need to ask questions about a codebase or understand code using a knowledge graph
- understand-anything:understand-dashboard: Launch the interactive web dashboard to visualize a codebase's knowledge graph
- understand-anything:understand-diff: Use when you need to analyze git diffs or pull requests to understand what changed, affected components, and risks
- understand-anything:understand-domain: Extract business domain knowledge from a codebase and generate an interactive domain flow graph. Works standalone (lightweight scan) or derives from an existing /understand knowledge graph.
- understand-anything:understand-explain: Use when you need a deep-dive explanation of a specific file, function, or module in the codebase
- understand-anything:understand-knowledge: Analyze a Karpathy-pattern LLM wiki knowledge base and generate an interactive knowledge graph with entity extraction, implicit relationships, and topic clustering.
- understand-anything:understand-onboard: Use when you need to generate an onboarding guide for new team members joining a project
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.prompt:
Read-only analysis task. Do NOT modify any file. Work in /Users/won-yechan/miru_github/agent-dashboard.

I need a complete contract of what dashboard/app.js references so I can rewrite dashboard/design-system.css without breaking styling.

Produce these lists precisely:

1. ALL DOM element IDs that app.js reads/uses via getElementById, querySelector('#...'), or string IDs. Run: grep -oE "getElementById\('[^']+'\)" dashboard/app.js and grep -oE "querySelector(All)?\('#[^']+'\)" dashboard/app.js and any `#...` selectors. Return the unique sorted list of IDs.

2. ALL CSS class names that app.js GENERATES in template strings / createElement / className / classList / class="..." within JS strings. Run: grep -oE 'class="[^"]+"' dashboard/app.js then split on spaces and produce the unique sorted list of every class token. Also catch classList.add(...) and className = '...' assignments. Return the unique sorted list of class tokens.

3. Which classes/selectors does app.js use with querySelector/closest/matches (i.e. classes it reads for event delegation, e.g. `.nav-item`, `[data-view]`, `[data-open-projects]`, etc.)? List every attribute selector (data-*) and class selector used in querySelector/closest/matches/delegation. Return unique sorted list.

4. The sidebar toggle logic: find the function that handles #sidebar-toggle click (likely toggleSidebar). Report EXACTLY which class name(s) it toggles and on which element (e.g. does it toggle 'collapsed' on document.body, on .app, on .sidebar?). Quote the 2-3 relevant lines.

5. Any data-* attributes app.js sets or reads on elements (data-count, data-view, data-panel, data-open-projects, data-reg-*, data-pub-*, etc.). Return unique sorted list of the attribute names.

Return all five lists directly in your final message as plain text (sorted, deduped). Do not write any report files. Keep it compact — just the lists and the sidebar-toggle quote.
